content:
The behaviour tree is a structured decision process that runs to completion on a unit of work (a message pair) and either produces an output or doesn't. Not a game AI tree — closer to the robotics application. The tree metaphor earns its place because the decision logic is genuinely hierarchical and failure/success propagation gives clean short-circuit behaviour.

Inputs: a message pair (agent turn, user turn, repo path, timestamp, session context)
Outputs: one of — write to Octowiki, append to questions log, append to rejected directions, discard

Tree structure:
Root (Sequence)
├── LoadProjectContext
│   ├── ReadOctowiki(relevant sections)
│   └── ReadRecentDecisions(last N entries)
├── PairAnalysis (Sequence)
│   ├── DetectRatification
│   ├── DetectDecision
│   ├── DetectRejection
│   ├── DetectOpenQuestion
│   └── DetectClarification
├── Selector: Act on strongest signal
│   ├── [Decision or Ratification] → ProposeWikiWrite
│   ├── [Rejection] → ProposeWikiWrite(rejected_directions)
│   ├── [OpenQuestion] → AppendToQuestions
│   ├── [Clarification] → UpdateProjectSummary
│   └── [no signal] → Discard
└── MarkProcessed

provenance.raw_agent: The root is a Sequence, not a Selector... A failure at LoadContext should re-queue, not skip to the next pair.
provenance.raw_user: The behaviour tree is the most architecturally interesting part... The tree structure: Root (Sequence) ├── LoadProjectContext...
